paper.title: Characterisation of high velocity stars in the S-PLUS internal fourth data release

review.summary: This paper characterizes 64 high-velocity stars (V_GC > 400 km/s) using Gaia DR3 astrometry and S-PLUS iDR4 12-filter photometry. It contains 5 internal tables: (1) S-PLUS filter specifications, (2) astrometric parameters for 64 HiVel stars from Gaia DR3, (3) CNN-derived stellar parameters (Teff, [Fe/H], log g) compared with SPHINX for 64 stars, (4) stellar radii from StarHorse and CNN log g for 64 stars, (5) chemical abundances ([Fe/H], [alpha/Fe], [Mg/Fe]) from GALAH DR3 and LAMOST DR8 for 11 stars. 9 external resources are referenced: survey homepages (S-PLUS, SDSS/APOGEE, Gaia, SEGUE), software repositories (Galpy, AGAMA), data archives (Gaia ESA archive, PARSEC isochrones), and infrastructure (DPAC consortium). No machine-readable data tables are directly provided with the paper; data is available upon reasonable request to the corresponding author.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Documents the 12 S-PLUS photometric filters used in the study, including central wavelengths, bandwidths, and spectral features covered.

columns.name: Spectral features
columns.meaning: Key spectral features covered by the filter

internal_tables.evidence: Table 1 listing the 12 S-PLUS photometric bands: u, J0378, J0395, J0410, J0430, g, J0515, r, J0660, i, J0861, z with their central wavelengths, bandwidths, and associated spectral features.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary astrometric data for the 64 HiVel sample stars: Gaia DR3 positions, proper motions, parallaxes, and radial velocities from both Gaia and spectroscopic surveys.

source_refs.caption: Astrometric parameters from the Gaia DR3. The last column reports the radial velocity from other spectroscopic surveys.
source_refs.label: tab:astrometric_parameters_splus

columns.name: v_rad (Spectroscopy)
columns.meaning: Radial velocity from spectroscopic surveys (GALAH DR3 a or LAMOST DR8 LR b)

internal_tables.evidence: Table 2 containing astrometric parameters for 64 high-velocity stars. Each row has Gaia DR3 ID, RA, Dec, parallax, proper motions, Gaia radial velocity with SNR, and cross-matched radial velocities from GALAH DR3 (7 stars) and LAMOST DR8 LR (4 stars).
internal_tables.comments: Core data table. Contains the full HiVel sample of 64 stars. Data sourced from Gaia DR3 with cross-matches to GALAH DR3 and LAMOST DR8.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Derived stellar parameters (Teff, [Fe/H], log g) for all 64 HiVel stars from the CNN model, compared with SPHINX-derived Teff and [Fe/H].

source_refs.caption: Stellar parameters derived with our CNN and with SPHINX
source_refs.label: tab:stellar_parameters_splus

internal_tables.evidence: Table 3 with 64 rows of derived stellar parameters. CNN columns: Teff, [Fe/H], log g (with systematic uncertainties of +/-115 K, +/-0.2 dex, +/-0.2 dex). SPHINX columns: Teff and [Fe/H] with individual uncertainties. SPHINX does not provide log g.
internal_tables.comments: CNN trained on ~55,000 S-PLUS+LAMOST common stars. SPHINX trained on ~7,000 S-PLUS+LAMOST+SEGUE stars. Values are photometrically derived, not spectroscopic.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Derived stellar radii for all 64 HiVel stars, computed from StarHorse masses combined with StarHorse log g and CNN log g separately.

source_refs.caption: Stellar radii of the high velocity stars.
source_refs.label: tab:mass_logg_rad

columns.name: Radius (SH)^a
columns.meaning: Stellar radius computed using StarHorse mass and StarHorse log g

columns.name: Log g (CNN)
columns.meaning: Surface gravity from CNN model (this work)

columns.name: Radius (CNN)^b
columns.meaning: Stellar radius computed using StarHorse mass and CNN log g

internal_tables.evidence: Table 4 with 64 rows. Masses from Gaia EDR3 StarHorse catalog. Radii computed in two ways: using StarHorse log g and using CNN log g. Table notes: a=calculated using log g from StarHorse, b=calculated using log g from CNN, *=sources with sh_outflag != 0 in StarHorse, **=variable star in Gaia DR3.
internal_tables.comments: Derived quantities. Authors caution that large uncertainties in log g make these radius estimates unreliable.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Spectroscopic chemical abundances ([Fe/H], [alpha/Fe], [Mg/Fe]) for 11 HiVel stars from GALAH DR3 and LAMOST DR8, used for chemical tagging to constrain stellar origins.

source_refs.caption: Metallicity, alpha-elements and [Mg/Fe] abundance from spectroscopic surveys.
source_refs.label: tab:stellar_abundances

columns.name: Survey
columns.meaning: Source spectroscopic survey (GALAH DR3 or LAMOST DR8 LR)

internal_tables.evidence: Table 5 with 11 rows (some stars have entries from both surveys). 6 stars from GALAH DR3, 5 from LAMOST DR8 LR. Star 3696393857329932672 appears in both surveys. Columns: [Fe/H], [alpha/Fe], [Mg/Fe] with uncertainties.
internal_tables.comments: Spectroscopic data from external surveys, not original to this paper. Used for chemical tagging analysis in Section 5.2.

external_resources.description: S-PLUS (Southern Photometric Local Universe Survey) project homepage. The survey providing 12-filter photometric data used as input for the CNN stellar parameter determination.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for S-PLUS survey in Section 1 (Introduction)

external_resources.comments: Survey website. S-PLUS iDR4 data is the photometric data source for this work.

external_resources.description: SDSS DR17 / APOGEE DR17 homepage. APOGEE DR17 data used to train and test the machine learning algorithms for stellar parameter estimation.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for APOGEE DR17 in Section 2 (Data sources)

external_resources.comments: APOGEE DR17 provides training labels and test data for the CNN and ANN models.

external_resources.description: Gaia ESA archive. Source of Gaia DR3 data including astrometric parameters and the external.gaiaedr3_spurious table used for quality filtering.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for Gaia archive in Section 3 (High Velocity Stars Selection)

external_resources.comments: Gaia DR3 is the primary astrometric data source. The external.gaiaedr3_spurious table (norm_dg flag) is used for quality filtering.

external_resources.description: Galpy documentation. Python package used for orbit integration and calculation of kinematic/dynamical quantities (actions, energies, angular momenta).

source_refs.context: footnote URL for Galpy in Section 4.1 (Orbit integration)

external_resources.comments: Galpy is used with the McMillan2017 potential for backwards orbit integration over 10 Gyr.

external_resources.description: AGAMA (Action-based Galaxy Modelling Architecture) GitHub repository. Used for orbit integration in a barred potential to investigate the influence of the Galactic bar.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for AGAMA repository in Section 4.1 (Orbit integration / barred potential)

external_resources.comments: AGAMA is used with the Sormani et al. (2022) barred potential with pattern speed 35.5 km/s/kpc and bar inclination 30 degrees.

external_resources.description: SEGUE (Sloan Extension for Galactic Understanding and Exploration) DR9 survey page. Training labels for the SPHINX neural network were taken from a combination of LAMOST DR7 and SEGUE DR9.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for SEGUE in Section 5.3 (Comparison with SPHINX)

external_resources.comments: SEGUE DR9 contributed training data for the SPHINX model alongside LAMOST DR7.

external_resources.description: PARSEC (PAdova and TRieste Stellar Evolution Code) web interface. Used to generate theoretical isochrones for metallicities -2.0, -1.5, -1.0, -0.5 dex shown in the H-R diagram.

source_refs.context: footnote URL for PARSEC in Section 5.4 (The Hertzsprung-Russell diagram)

external_resources.comments: PARSEC isochrones (Bressan et al. 2012) are used for evolutionary stage identification in the Kiel and CMD diagrams.

external_resources.description: ESA Gaia mission homepage. Acknowledged as the source of astrometric data used in this work.

source_refs.context: Acknowledgments section, acknowledging use of Gaia mission data

external_resources.comments: Standard acknowledgment URL for Gaia data usage.

external_resources.description: Gaia Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC) page. Acknowledged for processing the Gaia data used in this work.

source_refs.context: Acknowledgments section, acknowledging DPAC for Gaia data processing
